channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ex-Olympian charged over alleged reflecting pool damage
item.description: The Trump administration has indicted a former Olympian over damage to the National Mall's reflecting pool. A lawyer for David Hearn says he's innocent.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has indicted a former Olympian over damage to the National Mall's reflecting pool. A lawyer for David Hearn says he's innocent.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Russia's economy badly hit by Ukrainian strikes on infrastructure
item.description: Russia's economy is suffering from high inflation, an acute labor shortage and widespread fuel shortages amid sustained Ukrainian drone strikes on energy infrastructure.
item.content:encoded: Russia's economy is suffering from high inflation, an acute labor shortage and widespread fuel shortages amid sustained Ukrainian drone strikes on energy infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: How young people feel about American identity, on the nation's 250th birthday
item.description: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.content:encoded: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.dc:creator: Janet W. Lee

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Heat threatens U.S. 250th celebrations, Russian advances slow as Ukraine hits back, infighting risks derailing Democrats' House hopes.
item.content:encoded: Heat threatens U.S. 250th celebrations, Russian advances slow as Ukraine hits back, infighting risks derailing Democrats' House hopes.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Could infighting derail Democrats' hopes to win back House?
item.description: Democrats are hoping they can win back the House majority this fall, but fighting within the party could complicate their plans.
item.content:encoded: Democrats are hoping they can win back the House majority this fall, but fighting within the party could complicate their plans.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries addresses Democratic Party divisions, says Trump is the bigger issue
item.description: On the eve of America's 250th birthday, NPR's Michel Martin asks House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries of New York about the Democratic Party's midterm future.
item.content:encoded:
On the eve of America's 250th birthday, NPR's Michel Martin asks House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries of New York about the Democratic Party's midterm future.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: America 250: How Alaska's Indigenous residents were forced to harvest seals
item.description: When the U.S. bought Alaska from Russia in 1867, it took ownership of the Pribilof islands, home to the Russian-American Company's commercial fur-seal enterprise — and to the Unangax^ people who were forced to harvest the seals as "wards of the state." Indigenous residents continued to live under government supervision until 1983, when Congress finally transferred local control of the Pribilof Islands to the Unangax^ people themselves. From Theo Greenly of Alaska Public Media.
item.content:encoded: When the U.S. bought Alaska from Russia in 1867, it took ownership of the Pribilof islands, home to the Russian-American Company's commercial fur-seal enterprise — and to the Unangax^ people who were forced to harvest the seals as "wards of the state." Indigenous residents continued to live under government supervision until 1983, when Congress finally transferred local control of the Pribilof Islands to the Unangax^ people themselves. From Theo Greenly of Alaska Public Media.
item.dc:creator: Theo Greenly

item.title: Some states rolling back worker heat protections
item.description: The heat dome encasing much of the eastern U.S. is reviving concerns about protecting workers from the heat. Many states have laws in place but some, including Florida, have rolled them back.
item.content:encoded: The heat dome encasing much of the eastern U.S. is reviving concerns about protecting workers from the heat. Many states have laws in place but some, including Florida, have rolled them back.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Meszaros

item.title: Grieving Camp Mystic father advocates for camp protections
item.description: A father who lost his 18-year-old daughter in the flooding at Texas' Camp Mystic now advocates for greater protections at other camps for young people.
item.content:encoded: A father who lost his 18-year-old daughter in the flooding at Texas' Camp Mystic now advocates for greater protections at other camps for young people.
item.dc:creator: Dominic Anthony Walsh

item.title: Some great TV shows to sample this holiday weekend
item.description: If you're looking to stay in this holiday weekend, NPR has a list of unsung TV shows worth watching, including a spy thriller, a British comedy and a therapy reality show.
item.content:encoded: If you're looking to stay in this holiday weekend, NPR has a list of unsung TV shows worth watching, including a spy thriller, a British comedy and a therapy reality show.
item.dc:creator: Eric Deggans
